---
title: "Cornus transcriptome"
author: "Eugenia Nikonorova"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r, , error=FALSE, warning=FALSE }
busco <- read.csv("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/working_dir/busco.csv", head=TRUE, dec = ".", sep=";", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
busco$type <- as.factor(busco$type)
busco$db <- as.factor(busco$db)

install.packages("paletteer")
library(paletteer)

ggplot(data=busco, aes(x = db, y = data, fill = type)) +
geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge()) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position="bottom") +
  labs(fill = "") +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
  axis.title.y = element_blank()) +
  geom_text(aes(label=data), position = position_dodge(1.0), vjust=-1.0, color="black", size=3.5) + 
  paletteer::scale_fill_paletteer_d("colorBlindness::paletteMartin")
  #scale_color_manual(values = c("f47068", "ffb3ae", "fff4f1", "1697a6", "0e606b", "ffc24b"))
  #scale_fill_grey(start = 0, end = 0.7)
  #scale_fill_brewer(palette="Greys")
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=12, angle=45))

 
```
Analyse GO annotation and create a figure

```{r, error=FALSE, warning=FALSE }

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

go_df <- read.csv("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Cornus_sericea_transcriptome_SDH/Files/GO_terms_highest_level_per_transcript.csv")

# === Count number of genes per GO term and ontology ===
go_summary <- go_df %>%
  group_by(Ontology, GO_name) %>%
  summarise(NumGenes = n_distinct(Transcript_ID), .groups = "drop")

# === Optional: Keep only top N terms per ontology ===
top_go <- go_summary %>%
  group_by(Ontology) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = NumGenes, n = 15) %>%  # <- adjust N if needed
  ungroup()

# Plot GO terms by function  - one variant of plot
ggplot(top_go, aes(x = reorder(GO_name, NumGenes), y = NumGenes, fill = Ontology)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Gene Function Classification (GO)",
    x = "GO Term",
    y = "Number of Genes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# Best variant (for paper and diploma)
ggdotchart(top_go, x = "GO_name", y = "NumGenes",
           color = "Ontology",                                # Color by groups
          # palette = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"), # Custom color palett                      # Sort value in descending order
           add = "segments",                             # Add segments from y = 0 to dots
           rotate = TRUE,                                # Rotate vertically
           group = "Ontology",
            label = top_go$NumGenes, 
           dot.size = 6,                                 # Large dot size
           font.label = list(color = "black", size = 9, 
                             vjust = 0.5),               # Adjust label parameters
           ggtheme = theme_pubr()                        # ggplot2 theme
           ) +
  labs(title = "Gene Function Classification (GO)",
          x = "",
          y = "") +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.7, 0.9))


ggsave("GO_ontology_barplot.png", width = 9, height = 10, dpi = 300)


```


```{r, error=FALSE, warning=FALSE }
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# Read your KEGG analysis data
kegg_data <- readLines("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/Cornus_transcriptome/Annotation/gff3_annotation/KEGG_pathways.txt")

# Extract groups, descriptors, and counts
groups <- c()
descriptors <- c()
counts <- c()
current_group <- NA

for (line in kegg_data) {
  line <- str_replace_all(line, "[\u00A0\u200B\uFFFD]", " ")  # Replace special chars with regular spaces
  line <- str_squish(line)  # Remove extra spaces

  if (!str_detect(line, "\\(")) {
    current_group <- line
  } else {
    line_clean <- str_replace(line, "^\\d+\\s+", "")
    
    descriptor <- str_trim(str_extract(line_clean, "^.*(?=\\s\\()"))
    count <- as.numeric(str_extract(line_clean, "(?<=\\()\\d+(?=\\))"))
    
    groups <- c(groups, current_group)
    descriptors <- c(descriptors, descriptor)
    counts <- c(counts, count)
  }
}



# Create dataframe
kegg_df <- data.frame(Group = groups, Descriptor = descriptors, Count = counts)
kegg_df$Group <- as.factor(kegg_df$Group)
kegg_df$Descriptor <- as.factor(kegg_df$Descriptor)


# Top 30 Descriptors
top_kegg_df <- kegg_df %>% 
  arrange(desc(Count)) %>% 
  slice_head(n = 30)

ggplot(top_kegg_df, aes(x = reorder(Descriptor, Count), y = Count, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Top 30 KEGG Pathway Annotations",
       x = "KEGG Descriptor",
       y = "Number of Genes") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

# Add the Class column based on row indices
kegg_df$Class <- NA

kegg_df$Class[1:146] <- "Metabolism"
kegg_df$Class[147:175] <- "Genetic Information Processing"
kegg_df$Class[176:212] <- "Environmental Information Processing"
kegg_df$Class[213:247] <- "Cellular Processes"
kegg_df$Class[248:336] <- "Organismal Systems"
kegg_df$Class[337:426] <- "Human Diseases"

# Verify
head(kegg_df, 150)
table(kegg_df$Class)

# Summarize counts by Class and Group
summary_df <- kegg_df %>%
  group_by(Class, Group) %>%
  summarise(Total_Count = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Order groups by Class and Total_Count
summary_df <- summary_df %>%
  group_by(Class) %>%
  arrange(Class, Total_Count) %>%
  mutate(Group = factor(Group, levels = unique(Group))) %>%
  ungroup()

# Plot with improved readability
ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = Total_Count, y = reorder(Group, Total_Count), fill = Class)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
  facet_grid(Class ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "KEGG Pathways - Total Counts per Group and Class",
       x = "Total Number of Genes",
       y = "KEGG Group") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))


ggdotchart(summary_df, x = "Group", y = "Total_Count",
           color = "Class",                                # Color by groups
          # palette = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"), # Custom color palett                      # Sort value in descending order
           add = "segments",                             # Add segments from y = 0 to dots
           rotate = TRUE,                                # Rotate vertically
           group = "Class",
            label = summary_df$Total_Count, 
           dot.size = 6,                                 # Large dot size
           font.label = list(color = "black", size = 9, 
                             vjust = 0.5),  
          legend.title = "", 
           ggtheme = theme_pubr()                        # ggplot2 theme
           ) +
  labs(title = "KEGG Pathway annotation",
          x = "",
          y = "") +
   theme(legend.position = c(0.7, 0.9))

ggsave("KEGG_plot.png", width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)


class_totals <- kegg_df %>%
  group_by(Class) %>%
  summarise(Total_Count = sum(Count)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Count))

kegg_df %>%
  summarise(Total_Count = sum(Count)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Count))
sum(kegg_df$Count, na.rm = TRUE)


print(class_totals)

```


Count annotions

```{python}

annotated_gff = "/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Cornus_sericea_transcriptome_SDH/Files/annotated_final.gff3"  # your updated GFF3

# ---------------------------
# Counters
# ---------------------------
counters = {
    'mRNA_total': 0,
    'UniProt_best_hit': 0,
    'RefSeq_best_hit': 0,
    'NR_best_hit': 0,
    'EggNOG_best_hit': 0,
    'KEGG_KO': 0,
    'Ontology_term': 0,
    'any_protein_hit': 0  # counts if UniProt, RefSeq, or NR exists
}

# ---------------------------
# Parse GFF3
# ---------------------------
with open(annotated_gff, 'r') as infile:
    for line in infile:
        if line.startswith('#') or line.strip() == '':
            continue

        parts = line.strip().split('\t')
        feature_type = parts[2]
        attributes = parts[8]

        if feature_type != 'mRNA':
            continue

        counters['mRNA_total'] += 1

        attr_dict = {}
        for field in attributes.split(';'):
            if '=' in field:
                key, value = field.split('=', 1)
                attr_dict[key] = value.strip()

        # Count individual annotation types
        for key in ['UniProt_best_hit', 'RefSeq_best_hit', 'NR_best_hit', 'EggNOG_best_hit', 'KEGG_KO', 'Ontology_term']:
            if key in attr_dict and attr_dict[key] != '':
                counters[key] += 1

        # Count any protein hit once per transcript
        if any(attr_dict.get(k) for k in ['UniProt_best_hit', 'RefSeq_best_hit', 'NR_best_hit']):
            counters['any_protein_hit'] += 1

# ---------------------------
# Print summary
# ---------------------------
print("mRNA annotation summary:")
for k, v in counters.items():
    print(f"{k}: {v}")
    
```


Sum of annotation results

```{r}
# Create vectors
category <- c(
              "UniProt", 
              "RefSeq", 
              "NCBI NR", 
              "KEGG", 
              "GO", 
              "Unannotated")

count <- c(19496, 26681, 26903, 12326, 12766, 2372)

# Create data frame
anno_Cser <- data.frame(Category = category, Count = count)


ggbarplot(anno_Cser , x = "Category", y = "Count", 
           ylab = "Number of transcripts",  xlab = "", title = "") +
  geom_text(aes(label=Count), color="black",
            position=position_dodge(width=0.9), vjust=-0.25, hjust=0.5, size=3.5) +
  theme( legend.position = "none")
```



```{r}
#phylogenetic tree
BiocManager::install("ggtree")
library(ggtree)
tree <- read.tree("~/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/SDH_prot_seq/clustal_omega_aln-fasta.contree")
ggtree(tree)


info <- read.csv("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/SDH_prot_seq/info.csv", sep=";", header=TRUE)
info$GA <- as.factor(info$GA)
info$Substrate <- as.factor(info$Substrate)

ggtree(tree) %<+% info +
  geom_tippoint(aes(color=GA),  size=1.5) +
#geom_text(aes(label=node), hjust=-.3) +
  geom_tiplab(size=3, hjust= -0.1) +
 geom_label2(aes(x=branch, label=Substrate), size=0.2) 
 

install.packages("TDbook")
install.packages("ggmsa")
library(TDbook)
library(treeio)

AA_sequence <- read.fasta("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/SDH_prot_seq/clustal_omega_aln-fasta", type = "AA")
p <- ggtree(tree) + geom_tiplab(size=3)
msaplot(p, AA_sequence, offset=3, width=2)

ggtree(tree) %<+% info +
  geom_tippoint(aes(color=GA),  size=1.5) +
  geom_tiplab(size=3, hjust= -0.1) +
geom_label2(aes(x=branch, label=Substrate), size=0.2) 


tree <- read.tree("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/SDH_prot_seq/Multiple_alignment.aln.contree")
ggtree (tree) + 
  #geom_tiplab(size=4) +
#  geom_label2(aes(label=label))
geom_text(aes(label=label), hjust=-.3)
  
```





```{r}
library(ggmsa)
library(Biostrings)

protein_sequences <- readAAStringSet("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/SDH_prot_seq/clustal_omega_aln-fasta")
protein_sequences <- readAAStringSet("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/subset_alignment.fasta")
#protein_sequences <- readAAStringSet("/Users/eugenianikonorova/Desktop/Work/Plant_sequencing/SDH_prot_seq/Multiple_alignment.fa")

DQD_domain <- c(124, 126, 128, 155, 214, 241, 269, 271, 279, 291, 304)
# For each sequence, extract amino acids at positions 5, 9, 15
# Define valid amino acids (1-letter codes)
valid_aa <- "ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY"
# Extract amino acids at those positions for each sequence
aa_sites <- lapply(protein_sequences, function(x) {
  s <- as.character(x)
  aa <- substring(s, DQD_domain, DQD_domain)
  paste0(aa, collapse = "")
})

SDH_domain <- c(336, 338, 381, 385, 406, 407,421, 422, 423, 550, 578, 582)
# For each sequence, extract amino acids at positions 5, 9, 15
# Define valid amino acids (1-letter codes)
valid_aa <- "ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY"
# Extract amino acids at those positions for each sequence
aa_sites <- lapply(protein_sequences, function(x) {
  s <- as.character(x)
  aa <- substring(s, SDH_domain, SDH_domain)
  paste0(aa, collapse = "")
})

NRT_domain <- c(483,484, 485, 488, 522, 523)
# For each sequence, extract amino acids at positions 5, 9, 15
# Define valid amino acids (1-letter codes)
valid_aa <- "ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY"
# Extract amino acids at those positions for each sequence
aa_sites <- lapply(protein_sequences, function(x) {
  s <- as.character(x)
  aa <- substring(s, NRT_domain, NRT_domain)
  paste0(aa, collapse = "")
})


# Convert to named AAStringSet
aa_set <- AAStringSet(unlist(aa_sites))
names(aa_set) <- names(protein_sequences)
# Save to temp file
writeXStringSet(aa_set, filepath = "subset_alignment.fasta")




ggmsa(aa_set, color = "Chemistry_AA", 
      font = "DroidSansMono", char_width = 0.5, seq_name = TRUE)
ggmsa(aa_set, color = "LETTER", 
      font = "DroidSansMono", char_width = 0.5, seq_name = TRUE)

ggmsa(aa_set, color = "Shapely_AA", 
      font = "DroidSansMono", char_width = 0.5, seq_name = TRUE, ref = "AtSDH", consensus_views = T)



```
